summary: Merged a rough but useful partial decompilation base for All-Star mode in src/melee/gm/gmallstar.c. The PR replaced four previously stubbed functions with C bodies, added the All-Star opponent and round data tables, corrected the GALE01 data symbol split for gm_803DEBE8/gm_803DEC4C, and cleaned the static header to expose only the needed typedefs. decomp.dev reported substantial unmatched-item improvements for the new gmallstar functions and .data/.bss, with one regression in gm_801B60A4_OnLoad.

key_files.role: Main implementation file; added static All-Star data tables, gm_80490940 storage, includes for gmregcommon and lbaudio_ax, and C bodies for gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, and gm_801B5ACC.
Removed extern declarations and a commented gm_803DEBE8 initializer from the static header; added the AllstarRoundInfo typedef with start/count fields.
Corrected gm_803DEBE8 .data size from 0xD0 to 0x64 and added adjacent gm_803DEC4C at .data:0x803DEC4C size 0x6C.

what_changed: Replaced the placeholder comments for gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, and gm_801B5ACC with C implementations in gmallstar.c.
Defined static gm_803DEBE8_t gm_803DEBE8[25] in gmallstar.c with the All-Star opponent table values that had previously existed only as a commented initializer in gmallstar.static.h.
Added static AllstarRoundInfo gm_803DEC4C[26], with visible initialized round start/count entries, and updated symbols.txt to represent it as a separate .data object after gm_803DEBE8.
Added gm_80490940_t gm_80490940[5] in gmallstar.c and used it as runtime per-opponent/round status state from the new All-Star functions.

gm_801B5324 now builds opponent character/color arrays, calls gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable, configures lbDvd preload cache entries, sets the stage id, and requests character/stage audio through lbAudioAx calls.
gm_801B5624 now prepares StartMeleeData for an All-Star fight using gm_8017CE34, rule flags, saved percent/time from gm_80473A18, and special handling when arg0->idx is 0 or 0x60.

gm_801B59AC now records match result state into gm_80490940, saves player percent and frame count into global All-Star data, and conditionally transitions via gm_8017CBAC when gm_8017D7AC succeeds at idx 0x60.

gm_801B5ACC now prepares the rest/transition scene, marks beaten opponents into allstar->x76 using HSD_Randi slots, stores upcoming opponents in allstar->_94, queues remaining characters with gm_8016A998, calls gm_801B5324 for the next round, and installs fn_801B5AA8 as a rules callback.
gm_801B60A4_OnLoad changed its gm_80490940 byte-zeroing loop and removed PAD_STACK(12); decomp.dev reported this as a regression for that item.
smart_moves: The PR recognized that the old gm_803DEBE8 symbol covered two logical adjacent .data objects, splitting it into gm_803DEBE8 size 0x64 and gm_803DEC4C size 0x6C in symbols.txt.

Moving gm_803DEBE8's real initializer into gmallstar.c and removing the giant commented initializer from the header reduced header noise and made the file-local data definition match the static symbol scope.
Introducing AllstarRoundInfo with explicit start/count fields captured the structure of the newly split gm_803DEC4C table instead of leaving it as anonymous 4-byte data.

The new functions thread together All-Star state across gm_80473A18, gm_803DEBE8, gm_803DEC4C, gm_80490940, StartMeleeData, MatchExitInfo, DVD cache, and audio preload calls, giving later work a concrete control-flow base.

The implementations preserve matching-oriented details visible in the diff, including PAD_STACK in several new functions, explicit casts, repeated gm_8017BE84 calls, sentinel character value 0x21, and unrolled copy loops.
decomp_lessons: When a .data symbol's size looks too large, check whether it actually contains multiple adjacent tables; here gm_803DEBE8 was narrowed and gm_803DEC4C was added as a separate object.
Static headers should keep type declarations but not own file-local data definitions or large commented data dumps when the C file can define the actual static object.

All-Star mode logic relies on several still-poorly-named globals and fields; future work should reuse this control-flow base but improve names for gm_80490940, gm_80473A18 fields, allstar->x76, allstar->_94, and gm_803DEBE8_t members when semantics are confirmed.
Partial nonmatching C can be worth merging as a base when it raises function/data similarity significantly, but the PR itself and bot report show this was not a fully solved section.
Small changes made for one match can regress a nearby function: decomp.dev reported gm_801B60A4_OnLoad dropped from 87.40% to 80.20% after this PR's zeroing-loop/PAD_STACK changes.
naming_conventions: New descriptive typedef: AllstarRoundInfo with fields start and count.
Existing address-based names were preserved for not-yet-understood symbols: gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, gm_801B5ACC, gm_803DEBE8, gm_803DEC4C, and gm_80490940.
Unresolved struct fields stayed in the project's offset-style names, such as x0, x1, x2, x3, x54, x74, x76, x9C, and _94.
All-Star-specific placeholder types remained visible: UnkAllstarData, UnkAdventureData, gm_803DEBE8_t, and gm_80490940_t.
The PR used project-style subsystem includes and calls: gmregcommon/gmRegSetupEnemyColorTable, lbdvd/lbDvd_*, and lbaudio_ax/lbAudioAx_*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(12), PAD_STACK(16), and PAD_STACK(24) in new functions to influence stack layout.
Used manual initialization of small local arrays chars[3] and colors[3] with the sentinel value 0x21 for empty character slots.
Used an eight-entry unrolled copy loop plus a tail loop when copying opponent x3 values into chars, matching compiler-like loop structure rather than simplifying the code.
Kept many narrow casts between u8, s8, s32, CharacterKind, and InternalStageId to preserve sign/zero-extension behavior.
Preserved explicit rule bit manipulation, including data->rules.x20 &= 0xFFFFFFFFFFFBFCFFULL and multiple x0_*/x1_* bitfield assignments.
The OnLoad zeroing loop was rewritten through a char pointer and descending for-loop, but bot output marks this area as a regression, so it should be revisited before reusing the tactic.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported 1 regression: gm_801B60A4_OnLoad decreased from 87.40% to 80.20% (-40 bytes).
jurrejelle wrote: "Not the biggest fan on how this one turned out, but it's a base for others to work off of for now."
No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that improve or fully match gm_801B5324, gm_801B5624, gm_801B59AC, or gm_801B5ACC.
Search for a follow-up that fixes the gm_801B60A4_OnLoad regression introduced or exposed in this PR.
Search for later renames of gm_803DEBE8_t, gm_80490940_t, AllstarRoundInfo, gm_803DEC4C, and UnkAllstarData fields.
Compare later gmallstar.c revisions for safer naming around allstar->x76, allstar->_94, x74 percent carryover, and x9C frame/time accumulation.
Look for other PRs that split oversized .data symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt to model adjacent tables.
